_doc: pa11y-ci config for the Civil Rights History Project. Run with:
Requires no devDependency install -- npx fetches pa11y-ci + headless
Chrome on first invocation (~5-10 minutes the first time). Subsequent
runs use the cached install (~10 seconds per page scanned).
Targets the deployed staging site by default. To scan a local dev
server, run `npm run dev` in a separate terminal and replace the
Standard set to WCAG2AA matches the audit's stated compliance level
(see docs/ACCESSIBILITY.md). 'wcag2aaa' would be stricter than the
audit claims and would produce false positives against the brand
The runners array uses both 'axe' and 'htmlcs' so each issue is
reported through two engines; differences between them surface
edge cases that one engine might miss.

_ignore_doc: The color-contrast / 1.4.3 ignore is the brand red + cream
compromise documented in docs/ACCESSIBILITY.md. Brand red on
cream measures 3.05:1, which passes large-text but fails
to every body-sized usage, but pa11y/axe will still flag the
large-text usages that are intentionally compliant under the
confirms each remaining large-text red usage qualifies as
18pt+ regular OR 14pt+ bold. Remove this ignore once a future
design-system pass unifies on a single AA-compliant red.
